methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Oklo is a publicly traded nuclear energy company founded by Jacob DeWitte and Caroline Cochran, currently valued at $7.00B after raising over $500M in total capital. The valuation universe comprises 3 public peers and 5 private competitors in the nuclear energy sector, including publicly listed NuScale Power and Terrestrial Energy alongside private firms Nano Nuclear Energy, X-Energy, and Fuse Energy.

The peer-median approach was inapplicable due to insufficient narrow peer clustering. The nuclear energy sector contains only 3 same-stage public comparables globally, and filtering by adjacent development stage or comparable valuation tier yielded fewer than the 3 minimum datapoints required for statistical reliability. A broad sector-wide median would introduce substantial noise and dilute sector-specific signal.

Given the constraint on traditional triangulation methods, this valuation lacks quantitative anchor support from conventional multiples. The $7.00B mark reflects market pricing in a nascent, capital-intensive sector where peer comparability is structurally limited. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway timelines, unit economics at commercial scale, and relative technology risk across competing modular and advanced reactor designs. Any valuation revision should incorporate proprietary dispatch and cost-of-capital assumptions rather than reliance on peer multiples.
